ops.title: Local probe failures degrade to local gate, not fatal
ops.body: Deliberate design choice (2026-07-19, commit 0a01113): a presence probe that fails to run — e.g. `ioreg` SIGKILLed by its 2s bound when a darwinbg-clamped daemon starves under host load — no longer kills a ModeLocal key release or a CDP bridge open. `routesConsent` (internal/auth/release.go) returns (false, nil) on a probe error: the flight falls through to the local Touch ID gate, which still gates, trading perfect presence information for availability. Scope: requester-side route decisions only. A snapshot that was read but does not parse stays fatal (invariant break, not starvation), and approver-side probe failures keep classifying Unavailable (ModeApprover in broker.go, ApproveBridge in bridge.go) so mesh failover keeps working. Pinned by TestKeyLocalProbeErrorDegradesToLocalGate and TestReleaseBridge/probe_failure_degrades_to_the_local_tap.
